title: PCI-DSS 8.6 â€” Application and system accounts: interactive use of a service account
exclusions: Does NOT restrict the account; detects interactive (console/RDP/SSH) use of an account flagged service-only in the identity registry.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_8_6_svc_account_interactive' (every 10m) emits per event: user, src, dest, LogonType, account_type, to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI-DSS 8.6 Application and system accounts not used interactively â€” directly evidenced.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI-DSS 8.6 (Application and system accounts) is enforced â€” Splunk UC-22.11.98: PCI-DSS 8.6 â€” Application and system accounts: interactive use of a service account.

compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.98' running on index=windows EventCode 4624; index=linux auditd; service-account lookup service_accounts.csv., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted â€” SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Logging into a service-only account via RDP fires the UC within 10m.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A service-account login via LogonType 3 (network, non-interactive) is absent.

spl:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624 LogonType IN (2,10,11) earliest=-10m
| rename TargetUserName AS user
| lookup service_accounts.csv user OUTPUT account_type
| where account_type="service-only"
| table user src dest LogonType account_type
description: Detects an interactive session on a service-only account â€” direct PCI-DSS 8.6 violation, which requires application/system accounts not be used interactively.
value: Removes the common post-audit-finding 'we didn't know that account was used interactively' narrative by making the misuse visible in 10 minutes.
implementation: (1) Maintain service_accounts.csv with account_type tag; (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) hit opens a P1 and disables the account; (4) weekly IAM review.
visualization: Table of interactive service-account logins, time chart, single value 'events in last hour'.
knownFalsePositives: Admin password rotations performed by a service-account-using tool may be reported as interactive â€” exclude via tool's user-as-tool mapping.
grandmaExplanation: Removes the common post-audit-finding 'we didn't know that account was used interactively' narrative by making the misuse visible in 10 minutes â€” so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
â€˘ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
â€˘ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` auditd; service-account lookup `service_accounts.csv`..
â€˘ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 â€” Configure data collection
(1) Maintain service_accounts.csv with account_type tag; (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) hit opens a P1 and disables the account; (4) weekly IAM review.

Step 2 â€” Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624 LogonType IN (2,10,11) earliest=-10m
| rename TargetUserName AS user
| lookup service_accounts.csv user OUTPUT account_type
| where account_type="service-only"
| table user src dest LogonType account_type
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 8.6 â€” Application and system accounts: interactive use of a service account** â€” Removes the common post-audit-finding 'we didn't know that account was used interactively' narrative by making the misuse visible in 10 minutes.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` auditd; service-account lookup `service_accounts.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedâ€”rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

â€˘ Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
â€˘ Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
â€˘ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
â€˘ Filters the current rows with `where account_type="service-only"` â€” typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
â€˘ Pipeline stage (see **PCI-DSS 8.6 â€” Application and system accounts: interactive use of a service account**): table user src dest LogonType account_type


Step 3 â€” Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 â€” Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of interactive service-account logins, time chart, single value 'events in last hour'.
